text.title: Supply Chain Risk Checklist Spike Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real deployments of Supply Chain Risk Checklist Spike. Each ties a symptom to a root cause and a concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Library has 18k stars - adopted; xz-utils-style backdoor missed.
antipattern.root-cause: Stars-only evaluation skipped CVE, maintainer, release-age signals.
antipattern.fix: Score all 7 signals; recover MAINTAINER-COUNT=3, CVE-HISTORY=3 in the xz case.

antipattern.symptom: Audit returned clean Monday; xz CVE published Wednesday; team unaware until Friday.
antipattern.fix: Schedule daily refresh of CVE + release data; mark stale reports.

antipattern.symptom: SSPL'd dep blocked SaaS launch; rip-and-replace cost two weeks.
antipattern.root-cause: Custom / SSPL license adopted without legal review.
antipattern.fix: Score custom licenses as 3 always; require legal sign-off to override.

antipattern.symptom: Library audited 2 years ago; never re-evaluated; original maintainer abandoned.
antipattern.fix: Set decay_date = now + 90d for yellow, +180d for green; re-run report on decay.
